article_meta.title: News Law & the Courts Black Lives Matter Director Spent Millions in Donations on Homes, Shopping, Vacations, Indictment

reasoning: The summary is largely neutral and attributes allegations to prosecutors, avoiding the source article’s charged framing. Minor issues include echoing the term "shopping sprees" and compressing context about other BLM figures, omitting that Patrisse Cullors denied wrongdoing and was not charged.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral, attributed language ("allege," "prosecutors claim"). One phrase "shopping sprees" mirrors the article but is minor.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Most claims are sourced to the indictment; the line about "similar allegations" lumps figures together and under-specifies that one pled guilty and another denied wrongdoing.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Avoids subjective judgments; evaluative claims are attributed to prosecutors or the indictment.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Tone is measured; slight sensational echo with "shopping sprees," but overall restrained.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks beyond reported allegations.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No assertions about motives or intent.
custom_reasoning.slant: Includes broader negative context about BLM leaders without noting exculpatory detail for Cullors, tilting emphasis slightly.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits that Cullors was not charged and denied wrongdoing, which appears in the article.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focus matches the article’s focus on alleged misconduct without amplifying it.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No systematic preference for elite or grassroots sources.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Significantly more neutral than the source’s charged framing; allegations are clearly attributed.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Covers prosecutors/indictment and context but drops the denial/not-charged detail for Cullors; sources_represented mixes source types and context rather than all perspectives present.
